site.title: Prestige Electrical Contractor LLC
site.address: 7447 E Southern Ave, Suite 101, Mesa AZ 85209

logo.served_format: WebP (filename extension is .png but bytes are RIFF/WebP)
logo.description: Combo mark: a circular iconmark composed of a lightning bolt cut through the middle, left half red (cool-to-warm radial gradient ~#C01000/#800000 to highlight #E01000) representing Heating, right half blue (radial gradient ~#003060 to #00A0BB) representing Cooling/Electrical. Below the icon sits a bold, wide, slightly rounded geometric sans-serif wordmark 'Prestige' in solid black, with a thin uppercase tagline 'Electrical Contractor , Heating and Cooling' underneath. The lightning bolt + dual-color circle is a clear hot/cold electric/HVAC metaphor and is the single strongest brand asset on the site.

headings.source: Google Fonts (loaded via fonts.googleapis.com css2 href)
headings.note: Squarespace-default; no custom brand heading font — Manrope is the stock template pick.

body.source: Google Fonts (loaded via fonts.googleapis.com css2 href)
body.note: Used for paragraphs, links, buttons, and h1/h3 fallbacks — again Squarespace template default.

wordmark_logo_font.description: The word 'Prestige' in the logo is NOT Manrope or Poppins — it is a bolder, wider, lightly rounded geometric display sans (visually closest to Eurostile Bold Extended / Zekton / Good Times Bold family). This would be redrawn as SVG if the logo is preserved.

colors_in_use_on_site.note: The live site uses ZERO brand color. Every CSS custom property resolves to either #000, #FFF, #F5F5F5 (hsla(0,0%,96.08%)), or #D9D9D9 (hsla(0,0%,85.1%)). The accent token --accent-hsl is literally 0,0%,85.1% — a light gray. All brand color lives exclusively in the logo.

imagery.notes: Only ONE real brand photo exists on the entire site: a shot of Prestige's Mesa storefront with their fleet — 3-4 white Ford Transit Connect vans plus a Tesla Cybertruck, all wrapped with the red/blue lightning logo, parked outside their building which has the Prestige sign mounted above the storefront entrance. This IS ownable. Saved as luxury-watch-ad.jpeg (note: the CDN filename is 'Luxury Watch Ad(36).jpeg' which appears to be the contractor's Canva/Photoshop export name, not an actual luxury watch — it's just the fleet photo). The homepage 'hero' is the unsplash-image-5QLCohwVndQ.jpg — a generic modern home exterior, CLEARLY stock. No team photos, no jobsite photos, no customer photos, no completed-work photos. The About page reuses the same two assets. Favicon is Squarespace's default (assets.squarespace.com/universal/default-favicon.ico) — no custom branded favicon.

favicon.note: Squarespace default favicon — needs to be designed from the logo circle iconmark.

meta_brand.note: OG image is just the logo on transparent — not a framed social card.

voice.tone: Formal-to-neutral, trade-professional, low-warmth. Corporate vendor register rather than homeowner-friendly. Reads like a capability statement written for a builder partner, not for a homeowner searching for an electrician. Minimal personality, no humor, no first-name/owner voice, no emojis, no urgency words, no guarantees. Zero 'you'-voice copy. Almost entirely third-person about the company.
audience_signals: Primary: production homebuilders (tract + custom) and general contractors in the Phoenix 'Valley' — explicit language: 'trusted trade partner for some of the leading homebuilders in the Valley', 'Builder-Friendly Setup', 'Our builder partners appreciate our organized approach', 'Strong Workforce & Capacity', 'multiple rough and trim crews ready to deploy'.
Secondary: commercial clients — 'commercial projects as needed'.
Tertiary: homeowners — a distant third, only mentioned as 'home owners' alongside builders/contractors; no homeowner-focused CTAs, no residential service-page funnels, no pricing.
industry_vocabulary_to_preserve: rough and trim (phases of construction electrical work)
QC / Project Manager per active community
proof_points_on_site: Over 27 years of hands-on electrical experience
Long-term partnerships with leading national and local builders
copy_flaws: Typo: 'Prestige Electrical Condtractor LLC' (missing letter rearranged) appears in footer
Extra space before comma: 'Electrical Contractor , Heating and Cooling' in logo tagline
Thin content: homepage is ~5 bullet points; about page is ~200 words
No service pages (Services link appears in nav but leads to minimal content)
No testimonials, no case studies, no gallery of completed work

what_is_real: The logo itself: a strong, ownable combo mark with a clear red-meets-blue heating-meets-cooling metaphor via the split lightning bolt — a genuinely good concept worth preserving.
The dominant color story derivable from the logo: warm red (#C01000/#E01000) + deep-to-bright blue (#003060 -> #0080A0) + black. This is ownable and differentiated in the trades.
One fleet/storefront photo that genuinely shows Prestige branding in the real world.
Trade-partner voice and concrete operational proof points (27yrs, 18yrs, rough+trim crews, QC per community) that are defensible.
what_is_missing_or_fake: No custom typography — Manrope + Poppins are stock Squarespace template fonts.
No brand color is applied anywhere in the site CSS — the entire UI is black/white/gray.
No photography library beyond the one fleet photo — the 'hero' is Unsplash stock.
No homeowner-facing voice, no service pages, no testimonials, no case studies.
verdict.recommendation: PRESERVE the logo (vectorize/redraw it cleanly) and the red+blue+black color system it implies. INVENT everything else: typography pairing, photography direction (we need at minimum: jobsite shots, headshots, truck-in-driveway shots), copy voice, service-page structure, favicon, OG card, and a proper brand system. The logo gives us a real anchor — the business has genuine equity in the lightning-bolt-split-circle mark and the red-hot/blue-cool HVAC+electric metaphor — but everything around it needs to be built from scratch. This is not a rebrand; it's a brand-system buildout around a pre-existing, salvageable logo and one usable photo.
